status_detail: Phase 1 of H2V's Dunkirk gigafactory: 200 MW PEM electrolysis (2 x 100 MW units). Permits obtained November 2022. Construction underway in the Dunkirk port industrial zone. Part of the ZIBAC (Zone Industrielle Bas Carbone) decarbonization initiative. Phase 2 will add 300 MW. Project is a key supplier for the Dunkirk steel and heavy industry cluster.

data_management.narrative_summary: H2V Dunkerque Phase 1 is a 200 MW PEM electrolysis project in the Dunkirk port industrial zone, Hauts-de-France, France. Developed by H2V Industry, the project obtained construction permits in November 2022 and is the first phase of a planned 500 MW total capacity (200 MW Phase 1 + 300 MW Phase 2). The plant will produce 28,000 tonnes of green hydrogen per year for local industrial users including steelmakers and refineries, delivered via a dedicated pipeline through the ZIBAC low-carbon industrial zone. Total investment for Phase 1 is approximately EUR 250 million. The project is a cornerstone of Dunkirk's industrial decarbonization strategy.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Dunkirk port industrial zone

offtake.offtaker_name: Dunkirk steel cluster (ArcelorMittal, others), local refineries, heavy industry
